metadata.title: Build a Hydrogen Radial Probability Sim with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = P(r) curve and mode marker. CRIMSON = mean marker and annotation. INK for all Text.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

lines: — the perfect name for our AI-powered

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — plot the hydrogen one-s radial probability density P of r equals four over a-zero cubed times r-squared times e to the minus two-r over a-zero. Mark r at the peak and the mean radius. Label both in units of a-zero. The r-squared factor is not optional — leave it out and you answer the wrong question.

props.command:
claude "plot hydrogen 1s radial probability:
  P(r) = (4/a₀³) r² exp(-2r/a₀)
  mark r_mp = a₀  (mode)
  mark ⟨r⟩ = 3a₀/2  (mean)
  x-axis in units of a₀"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Find the line that defines P. It should read: four over a-zero cubed times r-squared times exp of negative two-r over a-zero. If the r-squared is missing, you have plotted the probability density at a point — not the radial probability. That is the single most common error in hydrogen atom calculations. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# hydrogen.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
a0 = 0.0529e-9   # Bohr radius in m

def P_r(r):                   # ← the formula
    return (4/a0**3)*r**2*np.exp(-2*r/a0)

# r_mp = a0         ← mode (where dP/dr = 0)
# <r>  = 1.5*a0     ← mean (∫ r P(r) dr)
# <r>  > r_mp  ✓   (right-skewed tail)

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the teal marker at the peak. It lands at r equals a-zero — the Bohr radius. The most probable position to find the electron in the ground state is exactly one Bohr radius from the nucleus. Second: the crimson marker for the mean. It sits at one-point-five a-zero. The mean is to the right of the peak. The tail pulls it. Two different numbers for the same electron, in the same state.

Iterate: claude — update hydrogen dot py — shade the area under P of r beyond the peak — that is, beyond r equals a-zero. Label the shaded region with its integrated probability. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The hydrogen atom was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update hydrogen.py:
  → shade area under P(r) for r > a₀
  → label the shaded integral value"

beats.narration_text: Medhavy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at Medhavy A-I. Medhavy dot com.
